missing_perspectives: Elderly Malaysians (60+): cashless pushed too fast; vulnerable to fraud and exclusion
B40: cashless 'transactional rather than transformative'; structural bottlenecks of low capability and uneven access
Women micro-entrepreneurs: distinct barriers in adopting tech in home-based businesses
Orang Asli: digitalisation could marginalise; isolation and literacy barriers
Refugees and stateless: largely excluded from formal financial systems
Youth under 30: 80% have gone cashless per Visa survey
Rural across all states (especially Sabah/Sarawak): infrastructure, operators, literacy
alternative_framings: Class divide masks ethnic divide — Bumiputera rich-poor gap wider than inter-ethnic
Fraud is consumer awareness/behavioural problem, not technical — BNM says majority are victim-authorised
Hybrid cash-digital may be desirable, not full cashless — push too fast risks excluding vulnerable
international_parallels: India UPI: Fraud Risk Indicator system flags risky mobile numbers in real time
India RBI: 2FA mandatory for digital payments with bank liability
Singapore SRF: shared responsibility for fraud among FIs, telcos, consumers
Singapore Money Lock: feature lets consumers lock funds against scams
Indonesia QRIS: 0% MDR for micro-merchants up to Rp500,000 per transaction
Kenya M-Pesa: courts held Safaricom liable for fraudulent withdrawals — duty of care precedent

key_missing_facts.fact: NSRC handled 146,167 complaints, froze 138,915 accounts, recovered RM34.53M in 2025; now 24/7 under direct police leadership
BNM Financial Inclusion Framework 2023-2026 shifted focus from accessibility to broader development outcomes including digital literacy and consumer protection
Only 46% of Malaysian MSMEs have integrated online finance and accounting tools
2023 e-cash credit (RM100 to 10M adults) was explicitly designed to educate public on cashless culture
Visa survey: 80% of Gen Z and Millennials in Malaysia have gone cashless; 70% of all consumers attempted cashless lifestyle

geographic_gap: Sabah and Sarawak treated as monolith — Sarawak has S Pay, Sabah planning BayuPay. FELDA settlements have unique cashless adoption pattern unaddressed.
overall_assessment: Solid foundation on urban-rural divide and fraud risks but misses critical gender, class, and most-vulnerable lenses. Lacks BNM Financial Inclusion Framework reference and NSRC operations. International parallels underdeveloped.
